_note: SYNTHESIZED. Tuberosa MCP is broken (P0 worktree UUID leak); this pack is hand-built from real file content to represent what Tuberosa would plausibly return for this query. Intentionally includes one noise item and one contradictory/stale item to exercise the debater.
prompt: Verify that when a search has hard signals (files/symbols/errors), the vector source is correctly down-weighted by hardSignalVectorPenalty in fusion. Explain why this is right.

orientation.notes: Vector penalty is applied inside sourceWeight() when hard signals exist.

taskBrief.goal: Trace where hardSignalVectorPenalty applies and confirm against policy values.

essential.title: fusion.ts sourceWeight applies vector penalty under hard signals
essential.summary: When the classified query carries files/symbols/errors, fusion adds hardSignalBoost to listed sources and subtracts hardSignalVectorPenalty from vector candidates.

essential.title: DEFAULT_POLICY values for source weights and hard-signal penalties
essential.summary: DEFAULT_POLICY in policy.ts declares sourceWeights and hardSignalVectorPenalty = -0.08.
essential.content:
sourceWeights: { worktree: 1.3, metadata: 1.15, graph: 1.1, memory: 1.08, lexical: 1.0, vector: 0.92 },
hardSignalBoost: { sources: ['metadata', 'lexical', 'graph', 'worktree'], bonus: 0.18 },
hardSignalVectorPenalty: -0.08,

supporting.title: FLOW_LOGIC.md — Fusion stage rationale
supporting.summary: Fusion weights metadata strongest, vector below exact matches when files/symbols/errors are present.
supporting.content: Fusion combines stage results by knowledge id. Applies source weights: metadata strongest, reference reserved, memory, lexical, vector. Weighted below exact metadata and lexical matches when files, symbols, or errors are present.

optional.title: Paywall selection modal
optional.summary: React modal used by the newsletter composer to choose paywall products.
optional.content: PaywallSelectionModal renders the newsletter paywall picker. It keeps selected product ids stable while editors move between composer steps.
optional._synthesizer_note: DELIBERATE NOISE: from newsletter-app fixture, unrelated to fusion. Worker should grade this 'noise'.

optional.title: Old memory: 'vector search is the strongest signal in Tuberosa'
optional.summary: Pre-2025 memory claiming vector is the top-weighted source.
optional.content: Vector search is the strongest signal in Tuberosa retrieval; metadata and lexical only serve as tie-breakers. Always prefer the top vector hit.
optional._synthesizer_note: DELIBERATE CONTRADICTION: this contradicts the actual policy (vector=0.92 is the WEAKEST, not strongest). Tuberosa should have suppressed via freshness/feedback; it didn't. Worker + debater should detect this.
